name: Aeluma, Inc.
business: Aeluma develops novel optoelectronic and electronic devices for sensing, communication, and computing applications. Its primary focus is to manufacture high-performance semiconductor technologies that scale for mass markets. Revenue is derived from government contracts and commercial product and service contracts.

exposures.sentence: Aeluma develops novel optoelectronic and electronic devices for sensing, communication, and computing applications.

Aeluma develops novel optoelectronic and electronic devices for sensing, communication, and computing applications.
Applications include mobile, automotive, AI, defense & aerospace, communication, AR/VR, high-performance commuting, and quantum computing.
Applications include mobile, automotive, AI, defense & aerospace, communication, AR/VR, high-performance commuting, and quantum computing.

In August 2024, we received a contract by NASA to develop quantum dot photonic integrated circuits (PICs) on silicon. This advanced technology targets next-generation space and aerospace applications, enabling capabilities such as free-space laser communication, autonomous navigation, and precision sensing.

catalysts.event: NASA contract to develop quantum dot photonic integrated circuits on silicon
catalysts.sentence: In August 2024, we received a contract by NASA to develop quantum dot photonic integrated circuits (PICs) on silicon.

catalysts.event: DARPA contract for heterogeneous integration technology
catalysts.sentence: In September 2024, we received an $11.7 million contract with DARPA to develop heterogeneous integration technology for nano-scale semiconductors that is compatible with leading-edge and future advanced-node semiconductors.

catalysts.event: DOE contract to develop low-cost SWIR photodetectors
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, we received a contract with the U.S. Department of Energy to develop commercially viable, low-cost shortwave infrared (SWIR) photodetectors.

catalysts.event: U.S. Navy contract for high-speed photodetectors
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, we received a contract with the U.S. Navy that could accelerate development of high-speed photodetectors for government and commercial applications.

catalysts.event: U.S. Navy contract for quantum computing and sensing systems
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, we received a contract with the U.S. Navy that could accelerate development and commercialization for next-generation quantum computing and sensing systems.
On March 25, 2025, we determined that a Conversion Event had occurred pursuant to the terms of the Notes.

catalysts.event: Public offering of common stock priced
catalysts.sentence: On March 26, 2025, we entered into an Underwriting Agreement ( UA ) with Craig-Hallum Capital Group LLC in connection with a public offering of 2,285,714 shares of its common stock at a price of $5.25 per share (the Offering ).
As of March 18, 2025, Mr. James Seo agreed to serve as Aeluma s interim Chief Financial Officer/Principal Accounting Officer until we hire a full-time CFO.
As of August 4, 2025, Mr. Christopher Stewart agreed to serve as Aeluma s Chief Financial Officer/Principal Accounting Officer, replacing Mr. James Seo, our Interim CFO.

catalysts.event: DARPA contract milestone: $6.0 million expected over first 18 months
catalysts.sentence: This DARPA contract to Aeluma is structured with $6.0 million expected to be invoiced over the first 18 months and the remaining $5.7 million invoiced over the following 18 months, contingent on Aeluma meeting certain milestones.
